# Atlantic Fintech Daily Briefing
### Monday, March 23, 2026

---

## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News / TechCrunch* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News / CNBC* — The collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse has left thousands of Americans unable to access their savings, highlighting critical risks in embedded finance infrastructure.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News / FF News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling growing Big Tech interest in leveraging open finance data for credit and payments.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News / Bits About Money* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News / Workweek* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly creative and niche credit card products as startups compete to differentiate in a crowded market.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, saying war will boost global inflation](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bank-of-canada-interest-rate-9.7132984?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada held its benchmark rate steady at 2.25%, citing the ongoing US-Iran conflict as a driver of global inflationary pressure that complicates the monetary policy outlook.

- **[Stock markets dip for another straight week as U.S. war on Iran continues](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/canadian-stock-market-close-friday-march-20-2026-9.7136842?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian equities fell for a second consecutive week as geopolitical tensions in the Gulf continue to weigh on investor sentiment and market stability.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News / FinLedger* *(Relevant to Canada's ongoing open banking journey)* — Canada's federal government labeled screen scraping as insecure and set an open banking implementation target, a foundational moment in the country's still-evolving consumer-directed finance framework.

- **[Hopper's biggest customer is bringing its travel platform in-house](https://betakit.com/hoppers-biggest-customer-is-bringing-its-travel-platform-in-house/)** — *BetaKit* — Montreal-based travel fintech Hopper faces a significant business setback as its largest customer announces plans to internalize the travel technology platform it had been licensing.

- **[Canada's population shrank last year — a first for the country, StatsCan says](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/canada-population-decline-first-time-9.7133643?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Statistics Canada confirms Canada's population declined for the first time on record, a demographic shift with long-term implications for financial services demand, credit markets, and fintech growth strategies.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This community resource catalogues harmful and exploitative fintech practices, offering a timely reminder for Atlantic Canadian ecosystem builders and credit unions about the importance of ethical product design and financial inclusion principles.

- **[As gas prices rise, ride-hail drivers feel especially pinched at the pump](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/gas-prices-rides-hailing-drivers-9.7132355?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Rising fuel costs driven by geopolitical conflict are squeezing gig economy workers across Canada, highlighting a growing need for embedded financial tools — such as flexible credit and earned wage access — to support financially vulnerable workers in Atlantic Canada and beyond.

- **[Xanadu set to go public as SPAC deal clears shareholder vote](https://betakit.com/xanadu-set-to-go-public-as-spac-deal-clears-shareholder-vote/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian quantum computing company Xanadu advances toward a public listing via SPAC, a development worth watching for Atlantic Canadian investors and ecosystem builders tracking alternative paths to capital for deep-tech and fintech ventures.

---

*📬 Atlantic Fintech Daily Briefing is curated to support the growth of fintech across Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, PEI, and Newfoundland & Labrador. Have a tip or story? Reach out to the Atlantic Fintech team.*
